t_ch: 小女子似乎与您…未曾相识？
t_en: I don't believe... I've met you before?

t_ch: 最近这批智械的芯片出了些存储故障，历史数据都丢了。抱歉抱歉。
t_en: Recently, there has been a storage malfunction with this batch of chips for Intellitrons. We've lost all the historical data. Sorry about that.

t_ch: 哎呀，我记得您。您就是上次游玩「救助情境体验卡（免费版）」的客人吧！
t_en: Ah, I remember you. You must be the customer who played the free version of the Rescue Simulation Experience Card!

t_ch: 感觉还行？这可是我们最近新推出的沉浸式服务——真实场景，自由选择，丰富互动，还有超高智能的实力演员，包您能完美代入！
t_en: How was it? This is our newly launched immersive experience — featuring realistic scenes, freedom of choice, rich interaction, and intelligent and skilled actors. Your perfect immersion is guaranteed!

t_ch: 难道她…只是在演戏？
t_en: Could she just be... acting?

t_ch: 当然啊。哼哼…看您的样子，怕不是入戏太深，真的动了感情？
t_en: Of course. Hohoho... Look at you. You were so into the act... Did you develop some real feelings?

t_ch: 哎呦，大可不必。就当是看个戏，戏中人戏外身，可得好好分清楚才行。
t_en: Oh dear, don't be. Just think of it as watching a play. You'll have to differentiate between the character in the play and their real self.

t_ch: 不过这也说明…您很满意对吧？行嘞，您喜欢就好！
t_en: But that also means... you're satisfied with it, right? Well, as long as you like it!

t_ch: 果然，我早就猜到了。
t_en: Sure enough, I guessed it.

t_ch: 就是演个戏嘛。情景和台词都有不少欠打磨的地方，别太较真哈。
t_en: It's just an act. There are still a few scenes and lines that need polishing, so don't take it too seriously.

t_ch: 不过您都看到了最后，应该很满意对吧？行嘞，您喜欢就好！
t_en: But since you've stayed till the end, you should be satisfied, right? Well, as long as you like it!

t_ch: 欢迎下次再来体验*豪华版*的内容啊。
t_en: You're welcome to come back to experience the *deluxe edition* of our show!

t_ch: 旅途罹患
t_en: A voyage through peril, fraught with woe,

t_ch: 闹街彷徨梦思迷。
t_en: Streets bustling, lost in a dream's soft glow.

t_ch: 寂海拾花。
t_en: A lone flower plucked by the forlorn sea,

t_ch: 淡影离群叹惜怜。
t_en: Faded shades depart, sighing with pity.

t_ch: 缘何起…
t_en: Where does destiny begin...

t_ch: 望何期…
t_en: When does hope reign...

t_ch: 开拓者老爷！
t_en: Boss Trailblazer!

t_ch: 老爷老爷！
t_en: Boss, boss!

t_ch: 老爷的功绩有目共睹！
t_en: Boss' incredible achievements are on full display for everyone to witness!

t_ch: 老爷的容貌举世无双！
t_en: Boss' looks reign supreme, unmatched by anything in this world!

t_ch: 老爷出手这么慷慨。
t_en: Boss is so generous.

t_ch: 当然要尽心爱戴啦！
t_en: Of course I will treasure it!

t_ch: 旅途罹患
t_en: A voyage through peril, fraught with woe,

t_ch: 闹街彷徨梦思迷。
t_en: Streets bustling, lost in a dream's soft glow.

t_ch: 寂海拾花。
t_en: A lone flower plucked by the forlorn sea,

t_ch: 淡影离群叹惜怜。
t_en: Faded shades depart, sighing with pity.

t_ch: 缘何起…
t_en: Where does destiny begin...

t_ch: 望何期…
t_en: When does hope reign...

t_ch: 旅途罹患
t_en: A voyage through peril, fraught with woe,

t_ch: 闹街彷徨梦思迷。
t_en: Streets bustling, lost in a dream's soft glow.

t_ch: 寂海拾花。
t_en: A lone flower plucked by the forlorn sea,

t_ch: 淡影离群叹惜怜。
t_en: Faded shades depart, sighing with pity.

t_ch: 缘何起…
t_en: Where does destiny begin...

t_ch: 望何期…
t_en: When does hope reign...

t_ch: 你正面对着一台略显落寞的取款机。在其他机器忙于喷吐苜蓿币时，只有它安安静静地站在原地。
t_en: You find yourself standing in front of an ATM that looks somewhat forlorn. While the other machines are busy dispensing Alfalfa credits, it stands quietly in its place.

t_ch: （尝试取款）
t_en: (Try withdrawing some coins)

t_ch: 你按照标准流程按动按钮。取款机疯狂颤动，但它似乎被什么东西卡住了*嗓子眼*，吐不出任何东西。
t_en: You hit the buttons following the standard procedure. The ATM trembles vigorously, but something seems to be *stuck* in its throat, stopping it from spewing anything out.

t_ch: 看来你无法通过常规操作获得钞票了。
t_en: Looks like you won't be getting cash through the conventional way.

t_ch: 你正面对着一台略显怪异的取款机。它似乎被什么东西卡住了*嗓子眼*，疯狂颤动却吐不出任何东西。
t_en: You find yourself standing in front of an ATM that looks slightly odd. Something seems to be stuck in its throat, stopping it from spewing anything out despite trembling vigorously.

t_ch: 看来无法通过常规操作获得钞票。或许你还有其他好点子？
t_en: Looks like you won't be getting cash through the conventional way. Maybe you should try another way.

t_ch: （轻轻地拍拍）
t_en: (Knock it gently)
(Rumble...)

t_ch: 铁皮罐子里发出空荡荡的回响。片刻后，几张{RUBY_B#信用点}苜蓿币{RUBY_E#}不情不愿地从出钞口落下来。
t_en: A hollow sound comes from within the metal casing. After a moment, several {RUBY_B#credits}Alfalfa credits{RUBY_E#} reluctantly drop from the dispenser.

t_ch: （狠狠地踹一脚！）
t_en: (Give it a ruthless kick!)
(Rumble!)

t_ch: 取款机发出痛苦的哀鸣。出钞口卡顿几下，紧接着，无数{RUBY_B#信用点}苜蓿币{RUBY_E#}喷涌而出！
t_en: The machine groans agonizingly. After a pause, countless {RUBY_B#credits}Alfalfa credits{RUBY_E#} gush out from the dispenser!

t_ch: （用球棒殴打！）
t_en: (Hit it with a baseball bat!)
(Rumble!!!)

t_ch: 取款机被球棒砸出一处巨大的凹陷，几声急促的警报后彻底停摆了。路过的行人对你投以谴责的视线。
t_en: The baseball bat leaves a huge dent in the machine. The alarm siren blares out for a while before the machine comes to a total halt. The passing pedestrians glance at you disapprovingly.

t_ch: 不管怎么说，这下你似乎是真的没法取钱了。
t_en: In any case, it seems like there's no way you can get your money now.

t_ch: （尝试维修）
t_en: (Try fixing it)

t_ch: 你尝试用那套三脚猫功夫修理取款机——谁能想到呢？你在贝洛伯格学到的那点技术居然在这里还能用上。
t_en: You attempt to fix the machine with your amateurish skills. Who would have thought that the stuff you learned in Belobog would come in handy one day?

t_ch: 拆开外壳…拿出零件…挨个擦拭…再次拼装……
t_en: Remove the casing... Take out the parts... Clean them one by one... Put them back again...

t_ch: 好了！取钞机完好如初！
t_en: Done! The machine is as good as new!

t_ch: ……等等，怎么多了个零件？
t_en: ...Wait. Why is there an extra part?

t_ch: 你正面对着一台可怜巴巴的取款机。你的蹂躏让本就故障的取钞机雪上加霜，现在直奔退休边缘。
t_en: You find yourself standing in front of an ATM that looks somewhat pathetic. Your tampering has made things worse for the already malfunctioning machine. Now, it is on the verge of going kaput.

t_ch: 但你还能为可怜的取款机先生做些什么呢？
t_en: What else can you do for Mr. ATM?

t_ch: （试着往出钞口塞点钞票）
t_en: (Try stuffing some coins in through the dispenser)

t_ch: 你将几张苜蓿币从出钞口中塞了进去。
t_en: You stuff some Alfalfa credits into the machine via the dispenser.

t_ch: 「叮呤哐啷——」有什么东西一路顺着管道叮当下落，落在出钞口的盘子里。
t_en: "Clink... clank..." Something clinks all the way down the pipe and lands on the tray of the dispenser.

t_ch: 在*咳出*这几枚硬币后，取款机发出满足的叹息，重新运作起来。
t_en: After *coughing* out several coins, the ATM lets out a sigh of contentment and gets back up and running.

t_ch: 它现在看上去与其他取款机别无二致，正常到有些无聊了。
t_en: Appearance-wise, it is no different from other ATMs. There is nothing special about it, so much so that it is a bit dull.

t_ch: 一只华美精致的水晶酒杯漂浮在半空中——围绕在它身边的是三个不同颜色的玻璃酒瓶。
t_en: A gorgeous and exquisite crystal glass hovers in midair, surrounded by three different bottles of liquor.

t_ch: 伴随着自不远处传来的欢悦旋律，酒杯和酒瓶们飘荡着、旋转着，似乎在自顾自地举办一场奇妙的无人舞会。
t_en: Along with the jolly music nearby, the glass and bottles float and spin as though they were having a dance party of their own.

t_ch: 然而，从它们各自摇摆、飘荡的韵律中，你还是发觉了暗藏在这幅梦幻景象之下的不和谐音……
t_en: Yet, from the way they sway their bodies, you can still discern a hint of dissonance lurking beneath the surface of this magical scene...

t_ch: 红色酒瓶笨拙地摇晃着，每隔几秒便试探性地向水晶杯靠近——然而后者似乎总能敏锐地察觉到它的意图，谨慎且精妙地掌控着距离。
t_en: The red wine bottle, despite its awkward movement, will inch closer to the crystal chalice tentatively every few seconds. However, the glass seems keenly aware of the red bottle's motive and will keep its distance cautiously with finesse.

t_ch: 金色酒瓶的律动最为欢脱，它似乎早已习惯了、并且在享受着这个游戏。它与水晶杯之间的距离不断地拉进、放远、拉进、再放远；尽管无言，但它们之间似乎早已达成了某种默契。
t_en: The gold wine bottle is the best dancer. It appears to be very experienced in attending such occasions and is having a good time. It approaches the crystal chalice and then pulls away from time to time. Although the two do not speak, they seem to have formed some sort of rapport with each other.

t_ch: 绿色酒瓶的舞步稳定而克制，相比另外两位，它看上去似乎更像是一个…旁观者。但从它偶尔的晃动中，你能感受到它内心被压抑的渴望——它也许只是习惯了忍耐。
t_en: The green wine bottle's moves are steady and restrained. Compared to the previous two, it behaves more like a... spectator. But in its occasional sway, you can sense the suppressed desire within. Perhaps, it has just grown accustomed to holding back its desires.

t_ch: 你顿悟了：看似一派和谐的表象之下，暗藏着一场水晶杯与三个酒瓶之间的情感大戏。
t_en: The realization dawns upon you — there is a love drama ongoing among the three underneath the peaceful facade.

t_ch: {F#女士}{M#先生}，{F#女士}{M#先生}！
t_en: {F#Miss}{M#Mister}! {F#Miss}{M#Mister}!

t_ch: 一个清脆的声音直接在你脑海里响起，每个音节都像是小巧的银勺轻轻敲击高脚酒杯的杯口时发出的叮当声。
t_en: A crisp voice rings in your head, each syllable resembling the clink when someone gently taps the rim of a tall wine glass with a small silver spoon.

t_ch: 没错，{F#女士}{M#先生}，您没听错——是我在和您说话。
t_en: That's right, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}. You heard it right. I'm speaking to you.

t_ch: 有什么事吗？
t_en: What is it?

t_ch: 我现在有些困惑……
t_en: I'm puzzled.

t_ch: 你好像还挺忙的。
t_en: You seem pretty occupied.

t_ch: 您说的也没错，我现在有些困扰……
t_en: You're right — I'm in a dilemma...

t_ch: 您瞧，我是一只高端定制的水晶酒杯。打造我的人说过，像我这样的酒杯必须被用来盛装最有价值，最能带给人美好享受的酒浆。
t_en: Look, I'm a high-end, custom-made crystal wine glass. The person who made me said that I should be used to hold only the most valuable and finest wine.

t_ch: 但您也看到了，现在我的身边围绕着三种酒。我能隐约感觉到，它们都希望能和我拉近关系，成为我的伴侣。
t_en: But as you can see, there are three types of wine around me. I can sense their intention to get to know me and be my partner.

t_ch: 我得向您承认，{F#女士}{M#先生}，我现在完全不知道如何是好。外人都说当局者迷，我想只有像您这样清醒的宾客能帮我这个忙了。
t_en: Truth be told, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}, I have no idea what to do. As the saying goes, "The spectators see the game better than the players." I believe only a level-headed guest like you can offer me some sound advice.

t_ch: 我到底接受它们中的哪位呢…您能和那几位酒瓶都聊一聊，然后给我一些建议吗？
t_en: Which of them should I choose? Can you talk to each of the bottles and give me some suggestions?

t_ch: 不巧还有别的事…
t_en: There's something else I need to attend to...

t_ch: 这样啊…好吧，那我只能再等别人来帮我这个忙了。
t_en: I see... Guess I'll have to seek help from others.

t_ch: 干脆三种混着喝。
t_en: Why not just drink them all?

t_ch: …不行，那绝对不行！我可不想变得和那些廉价的苏乐达饮料杯一样……
t_en: ...That's out of the question! I don't want to become as cheap as those SoulGlad glasses...

t_ch: 水晶杯的腔调里充满了鄙夷和嫌弃。
t_en: The crystal chalice's tone is laden with contempt and disgust.

t_ch: 行，我来帮你问问。
t_en: Sure. I'll help you.

t_ch: 您愿意帮我吗？真是太好了，您真是一位人{F#美}{M#俊}心善的{F#女士}{M#先生}。
t_en: Really? That's wonderful! You're so kind and {F#enchanting}{M#dashing}, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}.

t_ch: 它们三位就在旁边，不如您直接和它们聊吧？
t_en: The three of them are right there. Do you want to chat with them now?

t_ch: 你答应了帮水晶杯找到最适合它的伴侣——该从谁开始问起呢？
t_en: You agree to help the crystal chalice to find her perfect match. Who should you start from?

t_ch: 询问红色酒瓶。
t_en: Talk to the red wine bottle.

t_ch: 你将视线投向笨拙的红色酒瓶。
t_en: You shift your gaze toward the awkward red wine bottle.

t_ch: 你为什么要追求水晶杯？
t_en: Why are you pursuing the crystal chalice?

t_ch: …为什么？这算哪门子问题，伙计？
t_en: ...Why? What sort of question is that, buddy?

t_ch: 你再好好看看，我可是一瓶酒！一瓶酒想要找到一只完美的酒杯来盛装自己的酒浆，这难道不是天经地义的事吗？
t_en: Look at me. I'm a bottle of liquor! Isn't it the most natural thing for a bottle of liquor to desire a perfect wine glass that complements it?

t_ch: 我敢打赌，就算飞遍整个匹诺康尼都再找不到比眼前这位更…漂亮的酒杯了。我可没有退路，这回必须成功！
t_en: I'm sure I won't find another glass as... beautiful as this one before me in the entire Penacony. It's all or nothing for me this time!

t_ch: 给我讲讲你自己。
t_en: Tell me about yourself.

t_ch: 你一个贵宾，居然会对我们这种酒瓶感兴趣？还真稀罕。
t_en: I'm surprised a guest like you would be interested in a wine bottle like me. This is unusual.

t_ch: 行吧，既然你想知道，那我也不藏着掖着——实话告诉你，我的出身没什么特别的，就是一瓶街边杂货铺子都能拿出来卖的普通葡萄酒。
t_en: Fine, I'll tell you everything you want to know. My background is nothing extraordinary. I'm just a common red wine that you can find at any grocery store on the street.

t_ch: 别看包装有模有样，实际上那些有钱人或者酒客根本不会正眼看我一眼——
t_en: I might be well-packaged, but in reality, those rich folks and drinkers wouldn't even spare me a second glance.

t_ch: ——嘁，搞得他们好像都很懂酒一样！等真到了哪个宴会上，他们谁能分出来自己喝下肚的是什么玩意？
t_en: Tsk, they act as if they know a lot about wine! Can they even tell what they are actually drinking at those banquets they attend?

t_ch: 我不服气，我不相信自己生来就是被哪个流浪汉随手买去、没喝一半就被丢到小黑巷子里的命！所以我一路打拼，苦苦寻觅，现在终于让我找到了这个机会……
t_en: I refuse to accept my fate. I don't want to end up being bought on a whim by some homeless person and chucked away into a small, dark alley without even being finished! So, I've been working hard in search of a chance to make it big...

t_ch: 只要能得到水晶杯的青睐，我就能一举打入上流社会！我会让全世界看见，一瓶被人看不起的廉价葡萄酒也能靠着奋斗走到这一步！
t_en: By winning the crystal chalice's heart, I'll be on my way to joining the upper class of society! I want everyone to see that even a cheap red wine can make it that far through sheer hard work!

t_ch: …旁边那两个家伙，别想妨碍我！只要能借这个机会一飞冲天，我愿意使用任何手段，付出任何代价……
t_en: ...I won't let the other two guys stand in my way! I'm ready to do whatever it takes to make this work...

t_ch: 询问金色酒瓶。
t_en: Talk to the gold wine bottle.

t_ch: 你将视线投向欢脱的金色酒瓶。
t_en: You shift your gaze toward the cheerful gold wine bottle.

t_ch: 你为什么要追求水晶杯？
t_en: Why are you pursuing the crystal chalice?

t_ch: …哦？瞧瞧这是谁，一位…灰色的{F#女士}{M#先生}，穿着这城市里罕见的奇装异服——我喜欢您特立独行的风格。
t_en: ...Oh? Look who's here. It's a... {F#lady}{M#gentleman} in gray, wearing a bizarre costume rarely seen in this town. I like your unique fashion sense.

t_ch: 抱歉，朋友，您可能有些误会了…啊，我指的是「追求」两个字。
t_en: I'm sorry, my friend. I think you might have got the wrong idea... Oh, I mean about the "pursuing" part.

t_ch: 这算不上是「追求」，至多算是一种平等的联谊活动而已。您要问为什么，那当然是因为那位美丽的水晶杯早已倾心于我。
t_en: I don't consider myself to be pursuing the crystal chalice. We're simply getting to know each other as equals. Why? It's because that beautiful crystal chalice has long been smitten with me.

t_ch: 这不是自我感觉良好，{F#女士}{M#先生}，这是一个单纯的事实。相信您一定也看得出来吧？
t_en: This isn't self-flattery, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}. It's a fact. I believe you can see it too, right?

t_ch: 给我讲讲你自己。
t_en: Tell me about yourself.

t_ch: 我的出身？啊…相信我，这是一个乏味的故事，真的，您不会感兴趣的。
t_en: My background? Ah... Believe me, there's nothing to write home about. You won't be interested.

t_ch: 我是由全匹诺康尼排名前五的酿酒大师打造出来的一款黄金香槟，一般的逐梦客大概率一辈子都没机会碰我的瓶身一下。
t_en: I'm a bottle of golden champagne crafted by one of the top five winemakers in Penacony. The average Dreamchaser probably won't even get to touch my bottle in their lifetime.

t_ch: 我打出生起的唯一使命，就是被倒入一只完美、精致的酒杯中，供全银河最雍容富贵的名流品鉴。
t_en: Right from my birth, I've had only one mission — to be poured into a perfect and well-made glass for the enjoyment of the wealthiest elites across the entire cosmos.

t_ch: 这也是为什么那位水晶杯注定会和我成为一对——因为那就是我们被赋予的使命，「门当户对」大抵如此。
t_en: That's why that crystal chalice is meant to be with me. We're a match made in heaven, given our missions.

t_ch: 您当然可以提出异议，比如说这个故事*不够浪漫*之类的，但这就是事实。
t_en: You can argue to the contrary all you want, perhaps saying that the story "isn't romantic enough," but it's the truth.

t_ch: 如果它拒绝了我，接受了另外两位酒瓶的示爱，那么它们最后的结局注定会是…不幸福的。
t_en: If it turns me down and dates one of the other two guys, then their relationship is destined... to have a bad ending.

t_ch: 还请您相信我的判断，因为我看过太多类似的故事了。那些故事的结局往往都是相同的一幕：被摔得粉碎的玻璃渣，还有洒了一地、只能引来虫豸啜饮的廉价液体。
t_en: Trust me, as I've seen too many stories like this. They all tend to end with the same scene — shards of broken glass are scattered everywhere, and cheap liquid that only attracts insects is spilled all over the floor.

t_ch: 询问绿色酒瓶。
t_en: Talk to the green wine bottle.

t_ch: 你将视线投向严肃的绿色酒瓶。
t_en: You shift your gaze toward the earnest green wine bottle.

t_ch: 你为什么要追求水晶杯？
t_en: Why are you pursuing the crystal chalice?

t_ch: 我…我没有在追求，我只是在守护它而已。对，守护。
t_en: No... I'm not pursuing the crystal glass. I'm merely protecting it. Yes, it's protection.

t_ch: 请你不要胡乱猜测。
t_en: Please do not make wild speculations.

t_ch: 它似乎很不爱说话，而且还在努力克制自己的欲望。
t_en: It seems to be a bottle of few words and is still trying hard to restrain its desires.

t_ch: 给我讲讲你自己吧。
t_en: Tell me about yourself.

t_ch: 我…唉，好吧，我猜和你讲讲也无妨。
t_en: I... *sigh* Fine. I guess it's all right to tell you my story.

t_ch: 水晶杯可能不记得了…但我和它其实出自同一位工艺师之手。虽然品类截然不同，但我们诞生的时间隔得很近。
t_en: The crystal chalice might not remember it anymore, but the two of us were made by the same craftsman. Although we're different products, we were born around the same time.

t_ch: 这样说可能会让我显得很…难以形容，但我从第一天起就无法忘记它的样貌、它的身姿。
t_en: This might sound embarrassing, but I haven't been able to get it out of my mind since the day I saw it.

t_ch: 后来我被一位军官买下，跟着他一起在战场上奔波了许久。但直到被流弹击中的那天，他都没舍得打开我的瓶塞——我的朋友，愿他安息……
t_en: I was later bought by a military officer and followed him to the battlefield for a long time. However, he didn't open me even to the day he was struck by a stray bullet. My friend, may he rest in peace.

t_ch: 阴差阳错间，我又回到了匹诺康尼，又遇到了它。但我们已经分离太久，我还认得它，它已不记得我。我们的经历和身份都差太多，我不敢奢求什么。
t_en: By a twist of fate, I returned to Penacony and came across the crystal glass again. It no longer recognized me as we were separated for too long. Now, there's a huge gap between us in terms of our statuses. I'm not expecting anything in return from it.

t_ch: 我只是一瓶普通的朗姆，注定给不了它渴求的地位和认同。现在我想做的就只是默默地守护它，不让它被其它酒占了便宜…仅此而已。
t_en: Being just a bottle of rum, I'll never be able to offer it the prestige and recognition that it desires. I just want to protect it from being taken advantage of by other wines... That's it.

t_ch: 我知道水晶杯该选谁了。
t_en: I know which wine the crystal chalice should choose now.

t_ch: 水晶杯发出满怀期待的叮叮声，杯口向你倾斜了一些。
t_en: The crystal chalice clinks excitedly as it leans its rim toward you.

t_ch: 怎么样，{F#女士}{M#先生}？您觉得我该选谁呢？
t_en: How did it go, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}? Who should I choose?

t_ch: 选红色的葡萄酒。
t_en: The red wine.

t_ch: 那瓶红葡萄酒很有野心，也很有毅力，它应该很适合你。
t_en: That bottle of red wine is ambitious and determined. I think it is a great match for you.

t_ch: 选金色的香槟。
t_en: The golden champagne.

t_ch: 那瓶金色的香槟和你有着相近的出身和使命，你该选它。
t_en: That bottle of golden champagne shares a similar background and mission with you. You should choose it.

t_ch: 选绿色的朗姆。
t_en: The green rum.

t_ch: 那瓶绿色的朗姆愿意默默做你的守护者，它应该能给你幸福。
t_en: That bottle of rum is willing to protect you for nothing in return. It should be able to give you happiness.

t_ch: 原来如此，您是这么认为的……
t_en: I see...

t_ch: 我明白了——我一定会认真考虑您的建议，然后再做选择的。
t_en: I've got it... I'll take your opinion into account before making my decision.

t_ch: 十分感谢您的帮助，善良的{F#女士}{M#先生}。如果我的事后面有进展了，我一定会想办法告诉您的。
t_en: I appreciate your help, {F#Miss}{M#Mister}. I'll keep you posted if there are any updates on my side.

t_ch: …所以呢，接下来咱们该去哪场宴会？该让哪位贵宾老爷品品我的酒香？
t_en: ...So, which banquet should we attend next? Which esteemed guest should I offer a taste of my wine?

t_ch: 哦，我想起来了！鸢尾花家系是不是经常搞什么艺者庆功宴？咱们要不要去那儿露个脸？
t_en: Oh, right! Doesn't the Iris Family often throw celebratory banquets for artists? Shall we make an appearance there?

t_ch: 不对，感觉格局还是低了…要不干脆把目标定成谐乐大典吧！听说到时候全宇宙的名流都要聚在一块，那场面，绝对气派！
t_en: No, I think we should aim higher... How about we go to the Charmony Festival? I heard that all the famous figures from the universe would be there. It's definitely going to be grand!

t_ch: 你从刚才起就一直在说些有的没的…那我呢？你就没有什么话想对我说吗？
t_en: You've been rambling on about irrelevant stuff since earlier... What about me? Don't you have anything to say to me?

t_ch: …呃，怎么了，你不开心？但是你不是很喜欢那些场合吗？
t_en: ...Um, are you upset? Don't you love attending those occasions?

t_ch: …算了，我不想和你说话了……
t_en: ...Forget it. I don't want to talk to you anymore...

t_ch: 气氛变得尴尬了起来…你最好还是悄悄离开，假装没来过吧。
t_en: The atmosphere is getting awkward... You'd better take off discreetly and pretend you have never been there.

t_ch: 亲爱的，你觉得哪款水果片和我最配？
t_en: Honey, which fruit slices do you think go best with me?

t_ch: 当然是「热砂的时刻」特产的白柠檬片。不止和你的杯身很搭，和我的酒浆颜色也非常相称。
t_en: The lemon slices from Moment of Scorchsand, of course. Not only does it go well with the design of your glass, but it also matches with the color of my liquor.

t_ch: 你懂得真多…我还没去过「热砂的时刻」呢，下次你带我去嘛，好不好？
t_en: You're so knowledgeable... I've never been to Moment of Scorchsand. How about you take me there next time?

t_ch: 当然可以。不过你得做好心理准备，和「黄金的时刻」不一样，那里的逐梦客行事都比较…狂野，他们平时喜欢喝的也都是些粗制滥造的烈酒。
t_en: Sure. But you have to understand that the place is totally different from Golden Hour. The Dreamchasers there are more... unruly, and they enjoy drinking spirits of inferior quality.

t_ch: 唔…听你这么这么一说，我倒是不太想去了。我可不想沾上一身廉价威士忌的酒味……
t_en: Hmm... That's off-putting. I don't want to reek of the smell of cheap liquor...

t_ch: 哈哈，别怕。谐乐大典就快开始了，等到时候我带你去见见来自其它星系的上等名酒……
t_en: Haha... no sweat. The Charmony Festival is around the corner. I'll introduce the finest liquors from other galaxies to you there...

t_ch: …哇，真的吗？那我可太期待了，亲爱的！
t_en: ...Wow, really? I can't wait, darling!

t_ch: 它们聊的话题你恐怕是插不上嘴了…还是识趣点离开，假装没来过吧。
t_en: You can hardly chip in on their conversation... You'd better make your exit tactfully and pretend you have never been there.

t_ch: …你…不说点什么吗？
t_en: ...Don't you... have anything to say?

t_ch: …我…我不知道…该说些什么。
t_en: ...I—I'm not sure... what to say.

t_ch: 我…呃…我不太适应这种，场合。
t_en: I, um... I'm not used to attending this kind of... occasion.

t_ch: 场合？哪有什么场合…这不就是最普通的匹诺康尼露天聚会吗？
t_en: Occasion? What occasion... Isn't this just a common open-air party in Penacony?
Sigh...

t_ch: …别、别叹气，我…我会努力融入进去的，给我一点时间……
t_en: ...P—Please don't get upset. I... I'll try my best to blend in. Please give me some time...

t_ch: …好吧，可能是我太心急了。
t_en: ...Alright. I shouldn't be rushing you.

t_ch: 但你最好别花太久…我可不想被别的酒杯嚼舌根，说我每天都和一个无趣的酒瓶待在一起。
t_en: But I hope it won't take you too long... I don't want the other glasses to start gossiping about me, saying that I'm with a dull bottle every day.

t_ch: 不、绝对不无趣！我向你保证，我一定从下一秒就开始改正……
t_en: No, I'm not dull at all! I promise I'll get better starting from the next second...

t_ch: 你观察到水晶杯的杯口已经有些倾斜——顺着倾斜的角度望去，你看到了在远处的桌上摆放着的几瓶高档红酒。
t_en: You notice the rim of the crystal chalice is slightly tilted. Following the angle of inclination, you catch sight of several bottles of premium red wine on a table in the distance.

t_ch: …多么危险的信号。或许你该离开了…假装自己从没来过。
t_en: ...That's not a positive sign. Perhaps, it's time for you to go and pretend you have never been there.

t_ch: 红色的葡萄酒瓶躺在地上，瓶身上出现了许多裂痕——它瓶中的液体有一大半已经渗流出来，被染成深色的地砖缝隙勾勒出一片悲惨的事故现场。
t_en: The bottle of red wine lies on the floor, its body full of cracks. Most of the liquid has flowed out, staining the cracks between the tiles and outlining the scene of a tragic accident.

t_ch: 没人知道这里发生了什么，也没人会在乎。它梦想着摆脱平凡，并为此做出了太多努力，或许还干过些不能摆上台面的事…但现在那都不重要了。
t_en: No one knows or cares what has happened here. The bottle of red wine aspired to escape a life of mediocrity. For that goal, it made substantial efforts and probably even resorted to dirty tricks... Yet, none of those matters now.

t_ch: 如果还有一次机会，它还会继续做出相同的选择吗？还是说它会接受平庸、做一瓶普通人家餐桌上的红酒呢？你永远不可能知道答案了。
t_en: If it could live its life over again, would it have made the same choices? Would it accept its mediocrity and resign to being a bottle of red wine enjoyed by a modest family? That's an answer you'll never find out.

t_ch: 你接近金色酒瓶时，旁边的店员狠狠地瞪了你一眼。
t_en: When you approach the gold wine bottle, the storekeeper shoots you a glare.

t_ch: …这瓶「黄金香槟」只供展示，请宾客在欣赏瓶身时保持距离，切勿用手触碰。
t_en: ...This bottle of golden champagne is for display only. Please kindly maintain a distance and refrain from touching the bottle.

t_ch: 看来它之前没有胡说…这瓶酒确实价格不菲，一般人甚至连碰一下它都没有资格——这还是在梦境里。
t_en: Seems like the champagne wasn't boasting earlier... It really has a hefty price tag, and ordinary people aren't even permitted to touch it even in the Dreamscape.

t_ch: 哦，是您啊……
t_en: Oh, it's you...

t_ch: 金色酒瓶的声音突然又在你颅内响起，声线依然慵懒、高高在上。
t_en: The gold wine bottle's voice suddenly rings in your head. It still sounds as nonchalant and condescending as usual.

t_ch: 真遗憾，那位水晶杯没有选择我…不过这就是生活吧？总会有那么一两件事不会照着你想要的方向发展，倒也正常。
t_en: It's a shame that the crystal chalice didn't choose me, but c'est la vie, right? Sometimes, things just don't turn out the way you want them to. It's pretty normal.

t_ch: 这展览可真烦人，我就只能呆在这儿不动…下次联谊是什么时候？希望能再遇到一只和它一样漂亮——不对，最好是比它还漂亮的杯子。
t_en: This exhibition is so annoying, I have to be still as a statue the entire time... When is the next group dating session? Hopefully, I'll meet another glass that is as beautiful — or even better, twice as beautiful — as the crystal glass.

t_ch: 不知为何，你心中萌生出了一种想要把它砸碎的冲动。
t_en: For some reason, you feel an urge to smash it into pieces.

t_ch: 绿色的朗姆酒立在地上，周围歪歪扭扭地躺着些其它容器——你不知道这里发生了什么。
t_en: The bottle of green rum stands on the ground with other vessels strewn around it haphazardly. You have no clue what has taken place.

t_ch: 不许你们打它的主意…绝对不许……
t_en: I can't allow you to take advantage of it... None of you...

t_ch: 那个有些浑厚的声音再次在你脑中响起，眼前的绿色酒瓶似乎在极力压抑着自己的情绪。
t_en: Its thick, rich voice rings in your head again. The bottle of rum seems to be suppressing its emotions with great effort.

t_ch: 所有可能危害到它的…都由我来打倒……
t_en: I'll get rid of... everything that might harm it...

t_ch: 我要站在它背后，一直守护它…一直到…最后……
t_en: I'll have its back and protect it... until... the very last...

t_ch: 你想起一句不太文雅的流行用语：*某种动物**某个动作*到最后一无所有。但你不好评价什么，因为从某种意义上来说，这确实也是一种伟大爱意的体现。
t_en: You recall a rather inelegant popular saying: A simp always ends up with nothing. You don't want to make any comments about the rum's action. In a sense, it's an expression of great love.

t_ch: 在绿色酒瓶把你也当成威胁之前，你最好还是快点离开这里。
t_en: You'd better leave the place before the rum sees you as a threat.

t_ch: 不是说园区里没有人吗？现在的情况你又该怎么解释？！
t_en: Didn't you say there's no one in the park!? You owe us an explanation!

t_ch: 这…确实没有人啊。我也不知道为啥突然出现那么多怪物啊！
t_en: But... there really isn't anyone around. I have no idea why there are suddenly so many monsters here!

t_ch: 哎呀，现在扯那些都没用，赶紧想想怎么离开这里吧。
t_en: There's no point in dwelling on that now. We need to find a way to get out of here ASAP.

t_ch: 不如…我们分开来寻找出路？这样概率会大一些。
t_en: Why don't we... split up to look for a way out? We'll be more efficient that way.

t_ch: <size=28>绝对不可以！</size>
t_en: <size=28>NO WAY!</size>

t_ch: 我听说过那些怪物！好像是叫什么惊梦剧团，是家族的重点整治对象。落单的我们绝对没法幸存，搞不好还会被困在梦里、没法醒来……
t_en: I've heard of those monsters! They're called the Dreamjolt Troupe or something, and they have long been on The Family's radar. There's no way we can survive on our own. Even worse, we might be trapped in the dream and never wake up...

t_ch: 不过它们视力很差，我们只需要远远地绕过去就可以了。
t_en: One thing though, those monsters have poor eyesight. We just need to go around them from a distance.

t_ch: 没用的，我们回不去了…大家都会被困在这里，再也醒不过来……
t_en: It's not gonna work, and we won't be able to go back... Everyone will be trapped here forever and never wake up...

t_ch: 都这种时候了，你能不能别说这种丧气话啊！
t_en: Can you stop being such a negative Nancy!?

t_ch: 总之，大家先冷静下来吧。
t_en: Guys, calm down.

t_ch: 唉，要是有什么能骗过怪物的伪装就好了……
t_en: Gah, if only there's something we could use to disguise ourselves...

t_ch: 真见鬼，我们怎么变成了这副模样？
t_en: This is ridiculous. How did we end up looking like this?

t_ch: 亏你还是个导游，现在可好——看看你把我们导到什么鬼地方来了？
t_en: Aren't you a tour guide? Look at the place you guided us to!

t_ch: 这怎么能赖我？还不是你非要碰那什么电视机…而且这么一伪装，咱们不是更好躲过那些怪物了嘛。
t_en: How is this my fault? You were the one who insisted on touching the television... Also, this disguise might just help us slip past those monsters.

t_ch: 我…那你不也跟着进来了？
t_en: I, well... and you just had to follow me inside?

t_ch: 我…我那是担心，打算豁出去跟你同生共死，结果你连句谢谢也不说…幸好咱们只是变成了哈努兄弟，除了颜值进步以外并无危险……
t_en: I... I was worried about you, so I risked my life and went through this thing with you. Yet, this is how you thanked me... It's a good thing that we're only turned into Hanu — we're better-looking now and aren't in any real danger.

t_ch: 哎，不对啊，哈努兄弟不是只会发出「帅气的*哼*声」嘛？为什么我们还能说话？
t_en: Wait a minute. Isn't Hanu supposed to only make cool *hmph* sounds? Why can we still speak?

t_ch: 你在意那些干什么？这地方穿哈努皮套的演员也会说话。重要的是我们该怎么出去啊！
t_en: Why are you concerned with that? The actors who wear the Hanu costume here can talk too. Our priority now is to find a way out!

t_ch: 我想起来了！这个是克劳克影业推出的游乐设施。「黄金的时刻」街头也有，记得吗？
t_en: I've got it! This is a Clock Studios amusement ride. It can also be found on the streets in the Golden Hour. Ring any bell?

t_ch: 这里肯定存在另一台电视，那个就是出口…咱们去对面找找看吧。
t_en: There must be another television here, which would be the exit... Let's check out the other side and see.

t_ch: 谁在说话？你们到底谁是谁啊？妈妈，我分不清！
t_en: Who's speaking? Holy moly, I can't tell you two apart!

t_ch: 没想到，我们居然还能活着出来……
t_en: It's hard to believe we managed to make it out alive...

t_ch: 退钱！你这个导游，明明最开始的时候说自己有影城的VIP通道，结果带我们钻小洞入园不说，里面还全是怪物。
t_en: I want a refund! As our tour guide, you told us you had VIP access to the theme park, but we ended up having to sneak into the park. And what's worse, the park was filled with monsters!

t_ch: 哈，哈…这些其实都是我游览规划的一部分，几位没觉得非常惊险刺激吗？
t_en: Hmm, well... Those were all part of the itinerary! Hasn't this journey been a thrill?

t_ch: 你少来，明明里面只有惊吓。我也要退钱。
t_en: Just cut it out. There had been nothing but fear and shock. I demand a refund as well.

t_ch: 咳咳…咱们都是过命的交情了，还在乎那些干啥——今天的苏乐达，我请了！大家随便开怀畅饮吧。
t_en: Ahem... Since we've been through so much together, let's put all those things behind us! Today's SoulGlad is on me! Feel free to enjoy as many bottles as you like!

t_ch: 其实这趟也算不上那么危险啦，咱们一路上不是都没被怪物拦下嘛……
t_en: Actually, this trip isn't as dangerous as we think. At least we haven't found ourselves running into any monsters along the way, right?

t_ch: 我是不想再体验第二次了…还是逛商场更适合我。
t_en: Anyway, no repeats for me... Shopping malls are more my thing.

t_ch: 里面那是什么情况？
t_en: What's going on in there?

t_ch: 这…我也不知道啊。
t_en: I have no clue.

t_ch: 赶紧想办法带我们离开！
t_en: Hurry up and find a way to get us out of here!

t_ch: 我们回不去了…
t_en: We are going to be stuck here forever...

t_ch: 大家都冷静些…
t_en: Lets all calm down...

t_ch: 怎么回事？
t_en: What happened?

t_ch: 我们怎么变成了这样？
t_en: How did we end up looking like this?

t_ch: 你是谁啊？我分不清！
t_en: Who's speaking? I can't tell!

t_ch: 大家都别紧张…
t_en: Don't panic, guys...

t_ch: 没想到还能活着出来…
t_en: I can't believe I can make it out alive...

t_ch: 我要退钱！
t_en: I want a refund!

t_ch: 都是过命的交情了…
t_en: We've been through so much together...

t_ch: 洞穴里隐约传来了说话声，但寻常人的体型根本钻不进去。除非……
t_en: You hear a faint voice coming from inside the cave, but a regular-sized person could never get inside. Not unless...

t_ch: 瞧瞧你发现了什么，一只藏在广告牌里的折纸…大鸟，它的营养价值是牛肉的五倍。快偷偷地靠近它，给它一个惊喜。
t_en: Look what you've found! A giant... origami bird, hiding inside a billboard. Its nutritional value is five times than that of a big, juicy steak. Try sneaking up on it and giving it a shock!

t_ch: 该死，你忘了自己已经被缩小了——现在的你，身高还顶不过它的一根鸟毛。你真希望地上能长出一根豌豆藤，供你爬上去狠狠薅它一把。
t_en: Shucks, you completely forgot that you've been shrunk! At your current size, you're no bigger than one of its feathers. If only there was a beanstalk that would sprout from the ground and allow you to climb up and give it a good tug on the feathers.

t_ch: 说得好！百年三万六千日， 一日须倾三百杯！
t_en: That's right! A hundred years is just over thirty-six thousand days, and one must empty three hundred cups in a day!

t_ch: 大鸟似乎把你的动静当作了摇篮的韵律，悠悠然地发出呓语，和梦里的机巧鸟举杯对饮。
t_en: It seems that the giant bird feels your stirring movements as nothing more than the gentle rhythm of a rocking cradle. It let out relaxed murmurings and raise a toast to the cycranes in its dreams.

t_ch: 吃得个烂醉如泥好尽意！
t_en: Nothing beats a good feast and drinking to the death!

t_ch: 啾？早、早上好，你要一起来喝喝喝、喝一杯吗？
t_en: Tweet? M—Morning. Did'ya come to d—d—drink wiv me?

t_ch: 我来抓你归案！
t_en: I'm here to bring you to justice!

t_ch: 我可没做、做过什么坏事，我只是，在这、这里享受生活而已。
t_en: I didn't— I haven't done anything wrong, I just, I'm just enjoyin' my life here, that's all I've done!

t_ch: 要说坏事，工作就是我，干过的，最、最、最坏的事。不能偷懒难道不是世界上最大的坏事么？
t_en: If you wanna talk 'bout bad stuff... Workin' is the ba—baddest—worst thing I've ever done! Not bein' able to take a break all the time, dontcha think that's the baddest thing ever in the world?

t_ch: 啾，我知道了，你也是，来、来抓我回去干活的是不是？
t_en: Tweet, I get it now. You, you're— you came to make me work again, didn't you?

t_ch: 你已经醉了。
t_en: You're already drunk.

t_ch: 醉了？哈！我——我没醉，我怎么可能醉呢。
t_en: Drunk!? Hah! I—I'm not drunk, why would I— how could I be drunk?

t_ch: 不过，和大树的日子比起来，这儿的生活确实舒坦得跟醉了一样。
t_en: Then again, compared to the days at the Great Tree, the life here is almost as comfortable as bein' drunk!

t_ch: 等等，你、你不会是来逮我回去的吧？
t_en: Wait, y—you're—you've come to take me back, haven't ya?

t_ch: 想、想、想让我回去，门～儿都没有。工作那破事儿谁愿意谁干，反正我懒得干。
t_en: I—If, if you wanna get me to go back, you can think again! Who'd wanna do that workin' stuff? I know I can't be bothered.

t_ch: 大鸟不打算听你的解释，自顾自地在「巢穴」里摸索着什么，然后惊恐地「啾」了一声。
t_en: The giant bird has no intention of listening to your explanation. It just continues to feel around for something in its "nest" as if you aren't even there, before letting out a "tweet" fearfully.

t_ch: 它望向你，态度似乎发生了微妙的转变，哼，善变的大鸟。
t_en: It turns to you, and its attitude seems to have changed a little... Humph, what a fickle bird...

t_ch: 啾，朋、朋友，你，您现在有空么？
t_en: Tweet, m—my friend, do—do you have a minute?

t_ch: 有倒是有。
t_en: I do, but...

t_ch: 能给我带点东西过、过来吗？
t_en: Can you bring me something? Bring it here?

t_ch: 你先说说想干什么。
t_en: Tell me what you want me to do first.

t_ch: 方便的话…能帮我带点东西过、过来吗？
t_en: If you have time... Can you bring me something? Bring it here?

t_ch: （一声不吭地离开。）
t_en: (Leave without saying anything.)

t_ch: 英雄请留步，帮帮忙吧，我什么都会做的！
t_en: Please wait, hero! Help me, I will do anything!

t_ch: 也不知道是哪、哪、哪个酒鬼，我只是睡、睡了一觉，库存就被他喝空了。一想到世上还有这么丢人的家伙，我都、都替他脸红。
t_en: I don't know wh—which lou— lou— which lounge-lover could've done this but I had a— I took a little nap and now my stock is empty! I—I get second-hand embarrassment just knowing that there are such shameless people in the world!

t_ch: 据说，这朝露小屋珍藏的陈酿，喝了比星星的歌声还要美。那润泽的琼浆，那醇美的清香，哪怕是见到它那耀眼的光泽，都让我心旷神怡。
t_en: It is said that drinkin' the ol' beverages of Dawndew Hut is more beautiful than the songs of the stars. The glossy flow of the liquid, the sweet aroma, even just the dazzling luster can bring my heart calm.

t_ch: 只要你能给我带一瓶最香醇的美酒回来，我就，就，听你的，回、回去工作。
t_en: If you can bring me a bottle of the finest wine, I will— I will do as you say and g—go back to work.

t_ch: 反正都醉了，能拿瓶苏乐达来糊弄它吗？算了，还是不要跟只鸟一般见识为好。
t_en: It's already drunk. Couldn't you just fool it with a bottle of SoulGlad? Ah, best not. You don't want to stoop down to a bird's level.

t_ch: 三瓶酒互相倚在一起，能看出，它们的关系真的很好。
t_en: You see three bottles of wine leaning on each other. It is clear to see that they are very close.

t_ch: 请问，你们谁是这里最香醇的酒？
t_en: Excuse me, which of you is the finest wine here?

t_ch: 哈，你问谁我们三个谁最香醇？嘿，同胞们，居然有人想要用这么低劣的手段，破坏我们三瓶的完美情谊。
t_en: Hah, you're asking which of us is the finest of wines? Hey, brothers! You see what despicable methods they use to try and break up our perfect friendship!

t_ch: 等到我们三瓶争个酒死瓶破，你再来坐收渔翁之利，把我们统统都倒进下水道去，自己成为最香醇的酒，你一定打着这样的算盘吧！
t_en: You would wait for us to descend into battle and smash each other to pieces, then seize your moment to pour us down the sewers and become the finest wine yourself! That must be your plan!

t_ch: 很可惜，此一名杀三酒的计策被识破了。今我们三瓶酒，虽不是同年同月同日生，但也要同年同月同日死。
t_en: Unfortunately for you, we have seen through your triple alcohocide plot! We three bottles, though we may not have been born on the same day, month, or year, we shall die on the same day, month, and year!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我们三瓶的友谊坚不可摧！
t_en: Haha, our friendship is unbreakable!

t_ch: 我又不是酒。
t_en: I am not alcohol.

t_ch: 有没有一种可能，我是人，不是酒。
t_en: Have you considered the possibility that I am not alcohol, but a person?

t_ch: 黄，看呐，多么恶毒的计划，因为{F#她}{M#他}不是酒，所以不会被香醇的头衔所诱惑。
t_en: Yellow, do you see {F#her}{M#his} despicable scheming? Since {F#she}{M#he} is not alcohol, {F#she}{M#he} cannot be seduced by the title of "finest wine..."

t_ch: 要不是当初一气之下砸碎了那瓶苏乐达，又何至于如今这副田地。
t_en: If I had not let myself get carried away with emotion and smashed that bottle of SoulGlad, we would not be in this situation.

t_ch: 如今因我一瓶的过错，耽误了黄和小红的前程，你们去吧，最香醇的酒非你们莫属。
t_en: Now, because of my mistake, I have delayed both of your futures, Yellow, Red. Both of you, go on ahead, the title of finest wine belongs to you.

t_ch: 勿要自责，阿绿，我早知道你的香醇天下无敌，纵使走遍匹诺康尼，也不可能有比你更高档的美酒。
t_en: You must not blame yourself, Green! I have always known that you are finer than any wine in the land. I could travel the entirety of Penacony and find no finer wine than you!

t_ch: 黄，那我呢？
t_en: Yellow, what about me?

t_ch: 小红的香醇虽不如阿绿，但烈性十足，哪怕是举世无双的「钟表匠」来了，也定无法驾驭，饮上三杯便会醉倒。
t_en: Though your fragrance is no match for Green, you are extremely potent, Red! Even the incomparable Watchmaker would not be able to tame your fire! Three glasses and he would be cast to the floor, drunk.

t_ch: 有你这般评价，我愿一生一世做大家的小红。
t_en: Hearing such a touching assessment, I want to be your little Red forever!

t_ch: 不必不必，若有朝一日，你二瓶的才能得人赏识，被装进谐乐大典的酒杯里，勿要忘了曾有我这个黄便是。
t_en: Fear not, fear not! One day, your talents will receive the recognition they deserve and you shall fill the glasses of the Charmony Festival! When that day comes, just make sure that you do not forget about your friend, Yellow.

s_en: Green Wine Bottle and Red Wine Bottle
t_ch: 黄！
t_en: Yellow!

t_ch: 三瓶酒抱在一起，哭成一团。虽然你不知道酒瓶是怎么抱在一起的，但总之它们抱在了一起。
t_en: The three hugged each other tight and began to cry on each others' shoulders. Although you do not really understand how bottles hug each other tight, they did just that.

t_ch: 从今往后，只要我有一场宴会可饮，就绝对少不了你们两瓶的份。虽然不知道这位毒士是出自哪个酒厂的指示，但你放弃吧。
t_en: From this day forward, any banquet I attend shall have places for you both! I do not know which winery has sent you, foul villain, but you can give up!

t_ch: 我们三瓶都是这里最香醇的酒，你的阴谋绝不会得逞。
t_en: All three of us are the finest wine here, your plan shall not succeed!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我们三瓶的友谊坚不可摧！
t_en: Haha, our friendship is unbreakable!

t_ch: 你在想，要不把它们三瓶都交给小鸟算了，但一股强烈的好奇突然涌上心头。
t_en: You begin to think, why not just take all three of them back to the bird when suddenly, an irresistible curiosity surges through you.

t_ch: 它们三瓶到底谁是最香醇的？今儿个你就是要让它们三瓶比划比划，分出个一二三来。
t_en: Which one of them actually is the finest wine? You have decided that today, you will have them compare and rank themselves as a clear first, second, and third.

t_ch: 思考，仔细思考，三瓶酒的阵线绝非严丝合缝，只需一席话语，管叫它不攻自破。
t_en: Think, think carefully. There is no way that their defenses are impenetrable. All it would take is one word to get them fighting between themselves...

t_ch: 离间黄酒瓶。
t_en: Set Yellow against the others.

t_ch: 黄，其实我不久前偷偷听到了它们俩私下聊天，说了一些不太好听的话。
t_en: Yellow, I've actually heard the other two talking privately about you not long ago. They said some quite nasty things.

t_ch: 你不要妄图挑拨我们同胞三瓶的关系。
t_en: Do not try to sow discord between we three bottles' camaraderie!

t_ch: 说你的生产厂商不行。
t_en: They said your manufacturer is counterfeit.

t_ch: 就是个名不见经传的杂牌工厂，外包了点业务，才挂了名酒的牌子。
t_en: They said you're from a no-name producer that outsourced some work and stuck on a label from a high-end brand.

t_ch: 哈？！就它们俩那玩意儿还好意思笑我牌子？也不倒半杯出来照照自己是什么东西。
t_en: Wha—!? Those cheap toys dare mock my label? Why don't they pour out half a glass each and see what swill they're filled with!

t_ch: 我凭一酒之力，辛辛苦苦把它们从外面拉进朝露公馆，现在就是这么报答我的？
t_en: I popped my cork and worked myself to the glass to bring them into Dewlight Pavilion and this is how they repay me?

t_ch: 我就说它们这段时间老悄咪咪地，敢情是嫌弃我这老东西牌子杂了。
t_en: I knew they had been talking in secret whispers all the time lately. They just hate me because I am such an old vintage!

t_ch: 你给我评评理，就说真要论最香醇的酒，有我在，还轮得到它们？
t_en: You want me to tell you which of us is the finest wine? With me around, they are nowhere near!

t_ch: 离间绿酒瓶。
t_en: Set Green against the others.

t_ch: 阿绿，其实我不久前偷偷听到了黄和小红聊天，说了些不太好听的话。
t_en: Green, I've actually heard the other two talking privately about you not long ago. They said some quite nasty things.

t_ch: 呵，就你这骗汽水的鬼话，还能糊弄到本酒不成？
t_en: Hah, you think you can fool me with the same lies you would a cheap soda?

t_ch: 说你的原料不行。
t_en: They said you're made from poor ingredients.

t_ch: 是连苏乐达工厂都看不起的废品，低价回收来酿酒的。
t_en: They said that you're made from scraps that even SoulGlad Enterprise wouldn't use, recycled on the cheap to make wine.

t_ch: 什么？我原料是废品，那它们的原料就是不可回收垃圾。
t_en: What? I am made from scrap ingredients? Then their ingredients are trash that could not even be recycled!

t_ch: 亏我还和小红一起商量怎么给黄庆祝出厂日，它就是这么埋汰我的？
t_en: I was discussing with little Red how to help celebrate Yellow's bottling day, and this is how they insult me?

t_ch: 还有小红，我真是瞎了瓶盖了，能把这种酒视若己出。
t_en: And little Red! I must have had the cap pulled over my eyes to have seen her as my own vintage!

t_ch: 这位英雄，你就说，我这质量，它们有资格和我比试谁更香醇？
t_en: Hero, you think with my quality, they have any right to talk about whom is the finest wine here?

t_ch: 离间红酒瓶。
t_en: Set Red against the others.

t_ch: 小红，就前段日子，我偶然听到了黄和阿绿聊天，有些不太好听的话。
t_en: Little Red, I happened to hear Yellow and Green talking, they said some pretty nasty things about you.

t_ch: 你这厮，想说什么就快说。
t_en: Look, buddy, just say what you want to say.

t_ch: 说你是工业酒精。
t_en: They said you were industrial alcohol.

t_ch: 完全不能算是酒，谁喝谁脑子进水。
t_en: They said you don't even count as wine, whoever drinks you will end up with brain damage.

t_ch: 工业酒精？当初赚我入伙的时候，是谁夸我是三瓶里最辣的那个？
t_en: Industrial alcohol? When they tricked me into joining, who was it that said I was the most ferocious of us all?

t_ch: 这么多年了，我一直自甘屈身老三的位置，还不是因为我信任它们。
t_en: After all these years, I have always allowed myself to take on the role of the youngest because I trusted them!

t_ch: 好哇，信任就信出这么些东西。
t_en: Alright, that trust is shattered today.

t_ch: 今天我就要把酒瓶撕破，看看谁才是最香醇的好酒。
t_en: Today, I will tear open some bottles and we will see who is the finest wine of the three!

t_ch: 大计已成！
t_en: Mission complete!

t_ch: 在你的强烈攻势下，三瓶坚不可摧的友谊终于产生了裂隙。
t_en: Under your tireless offense, a rift has formed in the unbreakable friendship between the three bottles.

t_ch: 今天，我就要和各位一起，在这「朝露的时刻」，发烂发臭！
t_en: Today, in this Moment of Morning Dew, we finally will expose who is truly rotten and putrid together!

t_ch: 是啊，有的酒那些小心思，早就臭不可闻了。
t_en: That's right! It seems some wines have already allowed their thoughts to grow putrid!

t_ch: 谁是工业酒精？谁是工业酒精？你们今天就在这儿给我说清楚！
t_en: Who is industrial alcohol? Who are you calling industrial alcohol? Today, we will see who exactly that is once and for all!

t_ch: 看着三瓶酒吵得不可开交，你心中不由地升腾起一丝自豪的欢愉。
t_en: Seeing the three bottles locked in a fierce argument, you cannot help but become overcome with a sense of pride and joy.

t_ch: 英雄，多亏了你，让我看清了它们两个的真面目，今天你就来评评理，谁才是最香醇的酒。
t_en: Hero, thanks to you, I have seen their true natures at last. Today, you must be the judge and decide which of us is truly the finest of wines.

t_ch: 没错，真英雄慧眼识珠，就让这两个乡巴佬瞧瞧，真正的高档酒。
t_en: That's right, the hero has the discerning eye that will allow you two common bumpkins to witness what a high-quality wine truly is!

t_ch: 我现在谁也不服，就服你，来吧，是时候离开这里了。
t_en: I will no longer answer to anyone, except you! Come, it is time for us to leave this place.

t_ch: 选择黄酒瓶。
t_en: Select Yellow.

t_ch: 我就知道，英雄好眼力。
t_en: I knew it, you have a discerning eye, hero.

t_ch: 在另外两个酒瓶的鄙视里，黄兴奋地走进你的背包。它现在已经是最香醇的美酒，足以把世间一切美酒踩在脚下。
t_en: Under the disdainful glance of the other two bottles, Yellow excitedly strolls into your Inventory. Now, knowing that it is the finest of wines, it is prepared to crush all other imposters under foot.

t_ch: 选择绿酒瓶。
t_en: Select Green.

t_ch: 呵，各位，再也不见。
t_en: Hah, this is goodbye. See you again never!

t_ch: 在另外两个酒瓶的叫骂里，阿绿骄傲地走进你的背包。它现在已经是最香醇的美酒，所有的同类都入不了它的法眼。
t_en: As the other two bottles hurl out slurs and curses, Green arrogantly struts into your Inventory. Now, knowing that it is the finest of wines, no other is fit to be bathed in its warm gaze.

t_ch: 选择红酒瓶。
t_en: Select Red.

t_ch: 管谁叫工业酒精啊？谁叫工业酒精！
t_en: Who said I was industrial alcohol? Who is industrial alcohol now!?

t_ch: 在另外两个酒瓶的冷漠里，小红愤怒地走进你的背包。它现在已经是最香醇的美酒，可仍有团火在它的心头燃烧。
t_en: As the other two bottles fall into indifferent silence, Red angrily storms into your Inventory. Now, knowing that it is the finest of wines, the fire in its heart has been reignited.

t_ch: 啾，朋友，东西你找到了吗？快快快快给我！
t_en: Tweet, my friend, did you find what I wanted? Quick, quick, quick, give it to meeee!

t_ch: 酒瓶被一把抢了过去。
t_en: The bottle was immediately snatched from your hands.

t_ch: 啊！这酒高雅非凡，口感柔顺，像一股暖流涌上心头，真乃绝世罕有。
t_en: Ah! This wine has an incomparable elegance. Its flavor is smooth, like a warm current that rushes over my heart. Truly, a unique and rare sample!

t_ch: 能找到此等绝品，我果真没看错你。
t_en: You were able to find such a masterpiece, I knew I was right to trust you.

t_ch: 回味无穷，余韵悠长，感觉所有的烦恼都一下子消失无、无无踪了。
t_en: The aftertaste lingers and resonates endlessly. It feels like all of my cares and worries have melted without a trace.

t_ch: 啾，朋友，东西你找到了吗？快快快快给我！
t_en: Tweet, my friend, did you find what I wanted? Quick, quick, quick, give it to meeee!

t_ch: 酒瓶被一把抢了过去。
t_en: The bottle was immediately snatched from your hands.

t_ch: 哦！这酒品质绝佳，就像一位绝世的舞者，在我的心尖跳动。
t_en: Oh! This wine is truly a masterful work, it is like a world-class dancer casting their masterful steps along my heart.

t_ch: 现在的工厂都太注重品牌，反而忽视了酿造原料的质量，这样的极品，已经很多年没喝到了。
t_en: Factories these days are so obsessed with labels that they overlook the quality of the brewing ingredients. It has been many years since I have enjoyed a fine creation like this!

t_ch: 你能听见吗？它在我的胸膛里唱歌。
t_en: Can you hear that? It is singing a sweet song within my chest.

t_ch: 啾，朋友，东西你找到了吗？快快快快给我！
t_en: Tweet, my friend, did you find what I wanted? Quick, quick, quick, give it to meeee!

t_ch: 酒瓶被一把抢了过去。
t_en: The bottle was immediately snatched from your hands.

t_ch: 哈！这酒酒劲强韧，宛如有位格斗家一拳轰在了我的脑袋上。
t_en: Hah! This wine has a ferocious strength, drinking it is like a punch to the face from a great fighter!

t_ch: 单纯，真切，这就是纯粹的酒，纯粹的，纯粹的，烈！
t_en: Pure, sincere, this is pure alcohol. It is pure, pure... Strength!

t_ch: 我敢打包票，哪怕是猫头鹰老师来了，多半也撑不过三杯。
t_en: I would wager that not even Professor Owl themselves would be able to handle more than three glasses!

t_ch: 朋友，在我见过的人里，你绝对是品味最好的一个。
t_en: My friend, your taste is the finest of everyone I have ever met.

t_ch: 那是当然。
t_en: Of course.

t_ch: 这可是最香醇的酒——小心点，别喝那么急！
t_en: This is truly the finest of wines... Careful, do not drink it too quickly!

t_ch: 关于回去的事，嗯，等我再喝一口就回去，就一口。
t_en: As for me returning to work. Uh, I'll just have one more sip and then I'll go back, just one sip.

t_ch: 大鸟摇摇晃晃地飞走了…或许它只是找了个新的地方躲起来喝酒。
t_en: The giant bird took flight and headed off, swaying and weaving... Perhaps it has just gone to find another place to hide and drink wine...

t_ch: 在距离很近的地方，似乎有些古怪的动静，巨大，轻盈，甚至还有点…醉意？
t_en: Somewhere nearby, you hear a strange disturbance. You can tell that whoever is making the noise is huge, light-footed and perhaps even a little... drunk?

t_ch: 声音的源头仿佛就在头顶，可放眼望去，除了空荡荡的广告牌，什么也没有。
t_en: The sound seems to be coming from above you. However, looking up, you see nothing but an empty billboard.

t_ch: 就像是，飘着一团看不见的梦。
t_en: It's like there are invisible dreams floating above you...

t_ch: 可能是错觉吧。
t_en: Maybe it's just your imagination.

t_ch: 还好我们早就知道它心里根本没有我俩，这么想走，我们还求之不得呢。
t_en: At least we know now they never cared about us two! If they wanted to leave us that badly, they should have just said so!

t_ch: 哈哈哈，我们二瓶的友谊坚不可摧！
t_en: Haha, we two have an unbreakable friendship!

t_ch: 眼前的东西是什么？你愣在原地，大脑飞速处理起信息。
t_en: Just what is happening in front of your eyes? You stay stupefied on the spot as your brain rushes to try and make sense of the information.

t_ch: 答案很快揭晓——你碰上了一起性质恶劣的谋杀案。
t_en: The answer becomes clear very quickly... You have stumbled across a heinous murder!

t_ch: 从外形判断，死者是一盘盒式磁带。
t_en: Judging from the appearance, the deceased is a cassette tape...

t_ch: 凶手残忍地将它开膛破肚，大量的梦泡在尸体周边悬浮，昭示其短暂的死亡时间。
t_en: The murderer brutally disemboweled them... You can tell from the large number of dream bubbles floating around the body that the time of death was not long ago.

t_ch: 就在不远处，一只广告牌正在冥思苦想着什么。
t_en: Not far away, you see a billboard seemingly in deep thought about something.

t_ch: 年轻的朋友，很遗憾，你我的初次见面是在这种场景。
t_en: My young friend, I am sorry that our first meeting is under such dire circumstances.

t_ch: 自我介绍一下，我是匹诺康尼的新晋侦探——虽然还没有获得官方认证。
t_en: Allow me to introduce myself, I am Penacony's newest detective... Though I do not have official certification yet...

t_ch: 说来也惭愧，目前为止，我还没找到真正有用的线索。
t_en: Though I am ashamed to admit it, I've yet to discover any useful clues for this case...

t_ch: 犯人非常谨慎，既没有指纹，也没有任何与受害者争斗留下的痕迹。
t_en: The culprit was extremely careful. There are no fingerprints, nor are there any signs of struggle on the victim.

t_ch: 若非地面上散布的新鲜梦泡，我几乎要认为，凶手是在其他地点实施作案，然后用载具把尸体抛弃到了这里。
t_en: If it weren't for the fresh dream bubbles scattered across the ground, I'd almost think that the culprit committed the act somewhere else, and then used a vehicle to abandon the body here.

t_ch: 你有注意到那些带子吗？它们被故意缠在一起。如果犯人偷偷从里面剪去一段，也很难被发现。
t_en: Did you notice those tapes? It looks like they've been deliberately tangled up. If the culprit had secretly taken a cutting, it would not be easy to tell.

t_ch: 若真是如此，那段磁带大概就是他的动机。
t_en: If that's the case, that cassette tape was likely also the motive.

t_ch: 杀害目标，夺走证据，消除线索。犯人应该为今天的行动谋划很久了。
t_en: Kill the target, steal the evidence, and remove any clues. The culprit has likely been planning their operation today for a long time...

t_ch: 你还挺专业。
t_en: You're quite the professional.

t_ch: 过奖了，只是在这方面略有涉猎。
t_en: You're too kind. I've just done a little reading into this kind of thing.

t_ch: 靠你了，大侦探。
t_en: We're counting on you, great detective!

t_ch: 不敢当，只是个半吊子而已。
t_en: Oh no, I just dabble in this kind of thing.

t_ch: 如果可以，能否请您担任我的助手一职？毕竟我只是个广告牌，很多事情办起来都没有您那么方便。
t_en: Could I possibly ask you to be my assistant on this case? I'm just a billboard after all. A lot of things are a lot more convenient for you than for me.

t_ch: 我自知天赋愚钝，但若有您的助力，或许能为死者做到什么。
t_en: I know that I am a little slow-witted, but with your help, maybe I could at least do something for the deceased.

t_ch: 话都说这份上了。
t_en: Well, I'm already involved now.

t_ch: 一起加油吧。
t_en: Let's do our best.

t_ch: 有我那就稳了。
t_en: You can rest assured with me around.

t_ch: 感谢您的好心肠。我会再捋一捋线索，希望您能去周边的目击者那儿走访一遭，看看有没有重要的线索。
t_en: Thank you for your kindness. I'll try to piece the clues together. Would you be able to go and interview the witnesses around here and see whether they have any important leads?

t_ch: 爆米花和汽水夫妇都是这里的居民，应该会对死者有所了解。另外，有个汽水和爆米花的侦探组合也到了现场，或许能为我们拓宽思路。
t_en: The popcorn and soda couples are residents here, they should know something about our victim. Also, there's a group of popcorn and soda detectives that will be heading to the scene. Maybe they'll have some insights that can help us.

t_ch: 爆米花和汽水夫妇都是这里的居民，应该会对死者有所了解。另外，有个汽水和爆米花的侦探组合也到了现场，或许能为我们拓宽思路。
t_en: The popcorn and soda couples are residents here, they should know something about our victim. Also, there's a group of popcorn and soda detectives that will be heading to the scene. Maybe they'll have some insights that can help us.

t_ch: 瞧我说什么？那小子总有天得栽别人手里。死相这么惨，绝对是被仇家找上门了。
t_en: What're you lookin' at me for? That kid was gonna get what was comin' to him one day. Lookin' at this grisly scene, looks like one of his enemies finally got their revenge.

t_ch: 虽然他老在外面拈花惹草，也不至于搭上条命啊。
t_en: Just because he was always out flirting around doesn't mean he deserved to have his life taken, does it?

t_ch: 小小一盘磁带，好不容易摸爬滚打到这儿，还被黑心老板克扣工钱，落得个开除的下场，就算一时堕落，也是情有可原吧。
t_en: He was just a little tape that worked so hard to make it here, then he ended up having his wages cut by a cruel boss and getting fired! He may have gone off the rails for a while, but that's understandable, don't you think?

t_ch: 你搁哪儿听来的这版本，他自己编的吧？
t_en: Where'd ya hear that version of the story? He told ya that himself?

t_ch: 每天上班也不干活，就一条磁带转着放歌，老板实在忍不了才把他给踢出去的。
t_en: He never did any work. He'd just spin his tape 'n' play songs all day. The boss couldn't take it, so he gave him the boot!

t_ch: 据说，还因为录到了老板的什么把柄，私下从他嘴里翘出来不少钱。
t_en: Word on the streets is that he even secretly got a big payout from the boss 'cos he caught him sayin' somethin' on tape.

t_ch: 要我说，这案子保不齐就是那老磁带雇人干的。
t_en: If ya ask me, I reckon that tape's boss probably hired guys to take 'im out.

t_ch: 天杀的东西，大半夜的音量开那么大。要不是我懒得动，真想出来给他一榔头。
t_en: That darned kid, always playing songs super loud in the middle of the night! If I wasn't too lazy to move, I'd have gone out there and introduced him to my hammer.

t_ch: 亲爱的，你消消气，万一被谁听见，当心把你当凶手抓了！
t_en: Sweetheart, calm down. What if someone hears you and thinks you are the murderer?

t_ch: 咱们好好过自己的日子，不和小混混一般见识。
t_en: Let's just go on with our lives and not sink to those nasty gangsters' level.

t_ch: 我倒想消气，可那小杂种还借了我一千苜蓿币没还！
t_en: I'd love to calm down, but that little runt borrowed a thousand Alfalfa credits from me and hasn't returned it!

t_ch: 这笔钱，不是拿去赌，就是花在了和地痞流氓的花天酒地上。
t_en: If he didn't gamble that money away, he must have spent it getting drunk and partying with those local thugs!

t_ch: 赶紧回屋吧，昨晚他那下水道音乐闹腾得，我现在都还困着，气儿都跑没了。
t_en: Let's head back home. Last night, his terrible gutter music was so loud, I'm still exhausted. I've lost all my fizz.

t_ch: 你这一说那我更咽不下这气了，横竖得过去踹他小子两下。
t_en: Now you've wound me up again saying that! I should head over there and give him a good kicking!

t_ch: 好啦好啦，回家，别让大伙看笑话。
t_en: Alright, alright, let's go home. Don't let people see you causing a scene.

t_ch: 看来，大家还是缺少一双发现真相的慧眼。
t_en: It seems that they all lack the discerning eyes to see the truth.

t_ch: 是啊，无糖兄，口味太甜，糖甚至黏住了他们的脑袋。
t_en: Indeed, Brother Sugar-Free. They are all too sweet. The sugar in their minds has made their brains sticky.

t_ch: 论世间，也只有你能懂我，原味兄。
t_en: In this whole world, only you truly understand me, Brother Original-Flavor.

t_ch: 犯人并不是故意杀人，是驾车途中意外撞到了受害者。在不知情的前提下，受害者被车向前拖拽，所以磁带才会被扯到这么长。
t_en: The culprit did not intend to kill him. They just accidentally hit the victim with their car while driving. Unaware of what had happened, they continued to drag the victim forward with their car, which is why this tape had been stretched so far.

t_ch: 原味兄所言极是，但有一点需要补充。
t_en: You are correct, Brother Original-Flavor, but there is one thing I might add.

t_ch: 那是辆非常豪华的跑车，只有匹诺康尼最富贵的家伙才买得起的款式。
t_en: This car in particular was an extremely luxurious limousine, one that only the richest in Penacony could afford.

t_ch: 无糖兄真乃神探，这般细节，究竟是如何推断出来的？
t_en: You are surely a master detective, Brother Sugar-Free. Pray tell, how did you come to this conclusion for that detail?

t_ch: 很简单，我方才来的时候，亲眼看见它开了出去。
t_en: It is simple. I happened to witness them driving away when I arrived.

t_ch: 辛苦了，有您这番操劳，定能把犯人绳之以法。
t_en: Thank you. With your hard work, we will be able to bring the culprit to justice.

t_ch: 我看您曾在那两位爆米花身边停留，有听到什么消息吗？
t_en: I saw you standing by those two popcorns for a while. Did you overhear any information?

t_ch: 他和老板有过节。
t_en: He and his boss have had disagreements.

t_ch: 你如实把听到的对话转述给侦探。
t_en: You repeat the conversation to the detective exactly as you heard it.

t_ch: 品行不端啊。如此看来，这位老板长期遭他勒索，是有灭口的动机。
t_en: A bit of a problematic character, huh? It seems like the boss had been blackmailed by him for some time and had reason to want to silence him.

t_ch: 剪掉磁带，也是为了销毁死者掌握的把柄。
t_en: Cutting the tape is also to destroy the dirt the deceased held on him.

t_ch: 不过，现在还不是下结论的时候，让我再看看其它线索吧。
t_en: Although, we are not ready to reach a final conclusion yet. Let's keep searching for clues.

t_ch: 犯人就是他老板。
t_en: His boss is the murderer.

t_ch: 你夸张地讲述起犯人与老板的仇怨。
t_en: You exaggeratedly explain the boss's grudge against the victim.

t_ch: 独到的观点，但如果是他老板，会选择这种引人注目的方式吗？一时很难判断。
t_en: A unique perspective, but if it really was his boss, would they really have killed him in a way that draws so much attention? It's hard to judge for now.

t_ch: 别查案了，去玩吧。
t_en: Don't worry about the case. Just go and play.

t_ch: 朋友，现在可不是开玩笑的时候，死者还等我们还他一个公道呢。
t_en: My friend, now is not the time for jokes. The deceased is waiting for us to bring him justice.

t_ch: 那对汽水夫妇呢？他们有说些什么吗？
t_en: What about that soda couple? What did they say?

t_ch: 昨晚噪音很大。
t_en: They said there was loud music playing last night.

t_ch: 你把汽水夫妇的对话逐字逐句地转述给侦探。
t_en: You relay the soda couple's conversation to the detective word-for-word.

t_ch: 在高噪环境下，智慧生物的暴力行为确实更容易激发。毕竟也不是所有的犯罪都有明确的动机。
t_en: Intelligent life forms can easily grow violent under exposure to environments with loud noise. Not all crimes must have an obvious motive, after all.

t_ch: 但犯人的准备，我很难相信是一时兴起。音乐可能是他掩盖行踪的手段。还是再听听其他线索吧。
t_en: But with the preparation that the culprit must have gone to, it is hard to imagine that this was just a moment of passion. The music was likely a way to cover up his movements. Let's go and listen out for more clues.

t_ch: 犯人就是那瓶汽水！
t_en: The soda is the murderer!

t_ch: 你添油加醋地描绘了汽水先生的愤怒之情。
t_en: You embellish Mr. Soda's rage regarding the victim.

t_ch: 的确，如果那瓶汽水故意在您面前说这些话，很可能是和妻子一起演戏，好洗清身上的嫌疑。我再仔细想想。
t_en: Indeed, if the soda was deliberately having this conversation in front of you, it is likely that they were putting on an act with their wife in order to wash their hands of any suspicion. Let me think about this a little more.

t_ch: 别查案了，去玩吧。
t_en: Don't worry about the case. Just go and play.

t_ch: 朋友，贪玩虽无罪，但也要看时候啊。
t_en: My friend, though playing games is not a crime, there is a time and a place for it.

t_ch: 那两位跨种族的知己朋友呢？我看他们对案件似乎有所见解。
t_en: What about our different-flavored friends over there? I see they have some opinions on the case.

t_ch: 有辆豪车曾来过。
t_en: There was a luxury limousine that passed through here.

t_ch: 你把两位「大侦探」的推理转述给侦探。
t_en: You share the "great detectives' deduction" with the detective.

t_ch: 他们的猜想很有道理，也的确符合案发现场的环境。
t_en: Their conjecture does indeed make sense. It also fits with the circumstances found at the crime scene.

t_ch: 但这种地方，为什么会有豪车通过？只是路过？真撞到了死者，车主会毫无感觉？
t_en: But why would a luxury limousine be going through here? Were they just passing through? If they hit the victim, would the driver really be unaware?

t_ch: 这个推论有些生硬，不过我可能已经抓住了一丝头绪。
t_en: This theory is a little forced, but I might have grasped onto something.

t_ch: 犯人就是，你！
t_en: The culprit is... you!

t_ch: 照您这么说，也不无可能。作为侦探的我，确实是本案最难被怀疑的凶手。
t_en: With the case you've put forward, it is not impossible. As the detective on this case, I am the last person people would suspect as the culprit.

t_ch: 只可惜，我既没有作案时间，也没有犯罪动机。还是让我们回到正题吧。
t_en: Unfortunately, I had neither the time nor the motive to commit this murder. Let's get back to the problem at hand.

t_ch: 别查案了，去玩吧。
t_en: Don't worry about the case. Just go and play.

t_ch: 好提议，等把这个案子办完，我就抽空去放松放松。
t_en: A fine suggestion. Once this case is solved, I'll make time to get some good rest in.

t_ch: 关于这个案子，总有些东西在我的脑海里乱窜，想抓住，却每每与它们擦肩而过。
t_en: Some things about this case are rushing around my mind, but as soon as I reach out to put them together, they are gone again.

t_ch: 让我整理整理思路吧，朋友，我看您也累了，不如我们暂且别过。如果后续有消息，我也会通知您的。
t_en: Let me gather my thoughts, my friend. I see you are tired, too. Perhaps we should leave it here for now. If there is any further information, I will let you know.

t_ch: 广告牌侦探低着头，若有所思地离开了。
t_en: Billboard Detective hangs his head and leaves as if deep in thought.

t_ch: 这是一块无生命的广告牌——整个匹诺康尼恐怕只有你知道，他曾经以「广告牌侦探」的身份在这梦中活过。
t_en: This is a lifeless billboard... You are likely the only person in Penacony who knows that it once lived in the Dreamscape under the identity of "Billboard Detective."

t_ch: 你无从得知它为何会横尸街头…也许它找到了磁带凶杀案的真凶，结果惨遭灭口？
t_en: You have no way of knowing why they are now here, lying dead on the roadside... Perhaps they discovered the culprit behind the cassette murder case and were brutally silenced?

t_ch: 希望真相确实如你所想的那样，因为它生前是一位正直的侦探——它值得真相，也值得这个轰轰烈烈的结局。
t_en: Let's hope that the truth is indeed what you think because they lived as an honest and upright detective... They deserve the truth and a spectacular ending.

t_ch: 向你致敬，广告牌侦探！
t_en: We salute you, Billboard Detective!

t_ch: 谋财吗？还是仇杀？
t_en: Did they murder them for money? Or for revenge?

t_ch: 痕迹被抹除了。
t_en: The traces were wiped clean.

t_ch: 被剪下的磁带…
t_en: The tape cutting...

t_ch: 想带离现场的话…
t_en: If they wanted to take it away from the scene...

t_ch: 一幅哈努兄弟主题的挖洞合影立牌。你可以呼朋唤友，让他们把自己美貌的脸庞摆在合适的位置，与卡通立牌产生奇妙的化学反应，然后用相机记录下来。
t_en: A group photo board with face slots, featuring a Brother Hanu theme. You can call your friends and get them to place their beautiful faces in the appropriate slots to facilitate wondrous chemical reactions with the cartoon board. Then use your camera to record it!

t_ch: 机会难得，你想让你的朋友们试试看吗？
t_en: As you're here already, do you want to call your friends to give it a try?

t_ch: 当然！让我看看…
t_en: Of course! Let me think...

t_ch: 第一个孔位！你应该有不少符合这个身高要求的朋友。他们是谁？
t_en: The first slot! You probably have a fair number of friends that match this height, right?

t_ch: 瓦尔特…
t_en: Welt...

t_ch: 当然！和蔼的瓦尔特先生永远不会拒绝你。
t_en: Of course! Gentle Mr. Yang would never refuse you!

t_ch: 合影立牌啊…很久没看到过这种网红打卡点了。
t_en: A group photo board... I haven't seen a viral tourist spot like this for a long time.

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆…
t_en: Screwllum...

t_ch: 螺丝咕姆？天才俱乐部的天才——这可是位大忙人…但你也可以试着联系一下。
t_en: Screwllum? Genius of the Genius Society? Now that is a busy man, but you can try contacting him?

t_ch: 感谢你的邀请，{NICKNAME}。但愿我能为你留下难忘的回忆。
t_en: I appreciate your invitation, {NICKNAME}. I hope that this serves to create a wonderful memory for you.

t_ch: 这可真是让人意外。你永远可以信赖这个冰冷的男人…呃，他物理意义上的温度确实不算高，对吧？
t_en: Now that was unexpected! You can always count on that cold man... Ahem, well his biological temperature is not high, is it?

t_ch: 景元…
t_en: Jing Yuan...

t_ch: 罗浮将军——相信他一定不会拒绝到神策府外透透气的。
t_en: General Jing Yuan of the Luofu... I imagine he would not turn down a chance to get out of the Seat of Divine Foresight for a breath of fresh air!

t_ch: 呵…背着青镞跑到这么远的地方偷闲倒是头一次。罢了，相信她能处理好工作。
t_en: Heh... This is my first time running so far to slack off behind Qingzu's back. Never mind, I'm sure she can take care of things just fine.

t_ch: 银枝…
t_en: Argenti...

t_ch: 「纯美」骑士银枝——有传言他现在正在匹诺康尼游玩，或许是个不错的主意。
t_en: The Knight of Beauty, Argenti... Rumor has it he is currently having fun in Penacony. That might not be a bad idea!

t_ch: 啊，我的挚友！没想到你竟愿意邀请我前来合影…我一定不辱使命！
t_en: Ah, my dear friend! I never would have thought you would invite me for a group picture. I shall not fail my mission!

t_ch: 真理医生…
t_en: Dr. Ratio...

t_ch: 维里塔斯•拉帝奥教授？这位可不是说合影就合影的男人…但不妨试试看。
t_en: Professor Veritas Ratio? He's not the kind of person that will just take a group picture when asked... But why not give it a try?

t_ch: 他根本没接你的电话，短信也没回复——教授或许正在上课，还是算了吧。
t_en: He never even picked up the phone. He didn't reply to your message, either... Forget it. Maybe he's teaching a class right now.

t_ch: 我的朋友很少…
t_en: I don't have many friends...

t_ch: 噢，真可惜…那你快去多交点朋友吧。
t_en: Ah, that's a shame... Better go and make some more friends, then!

t_ch: 接下来是第二个孔位。符合这个要求的人可不多，你得好好想想了。
t_en: Next is the second slot. There aren't many people that would meet the requirements for this one. You'll need to think carefully.

t_ch: 青雀…
t_en: Qingque...

t_ch: 这位卜者一定很乐于接受你的邀请——尤其是上班时间内——但希望那位太卜能放她一马。
t_en: I'm sure the diviner would be more than happy to accept your invitation... Especially if it is during work hours... Let's just hope the Master Diviner lets her get away for a moment.

t_ch: 我来啦！哟，正好四个孔啊…来一盘帝垣琼玉吗？
t_en: I'm here! Ah, there are exactly four slots... Fancy a game of Celestial Jade?

t_ch: 白露…
t_en: Bailu...

t_ch: 如不出意外，这位持明女孩现在应该在丹鼎司深宫中仰望天空…她真的有办法溜之大吉吗？
t_en: If everything is as usual, the young Vidyadhara girl is most likely at the Alchemy Commission depths, looking at the sky... Do you think she'll be able to sneak away?

t_ch: 小菜一碟！这就是传说中的美梦乐园吗？真厉害……
t_en: Piece of cake! So this is the legendary Sweetdream Paradise? Amazing...

t_ch: …不愧是她。
t_en: ...We should expect no less of her...

t_ch: 银狼…
t_en: Silver Wolf...

t_ch: 邀请星核猎手？这可真是个绝妙的主意。
t_en: Invite a Stellaron Hunter? That's an amazing idea!

t_ch: 「很忙，在团战，」她听起来既咬牙切齿又敷衍，「下次一定。」看来你今天是没机会了。
t_en: "I'm busy, in a raid." She sounds like she's speaking through gritted teeth and trying to get the conversation finished quickly. "Next time for sure." Looks like you're out of luck for today.

t_ch: 黑塔…
t_en: Herta...

t_ch: 黑塔女士…哈，要是能请得动她，算你厉害。
t_en: Madam Herta... Ah, I'd like to see you trying to get her to come...

t_ch: 果然，她没接电话。意料之内。
t_en: As expected, she didn't pick up the phone. No surprise there.

t_ch: 你还真是锲而不舍。但黑塔就是黑塔，你明白她是怎样的一位奇女子……
t_en: You sure are persistent. But, Herta is Herta. You know what kind of quirky lady she is...

t_ch: …一阵漫长的忙音后，你期待中的声音果然没有响起。这很黑塔。
t_en: ...After what seemed like endless ringing, the voice you were waiting for never came to the phone. Typical Herta.

t_ch: 收手吧，朋友。黑塔女士是绝对不可能……
t_en: Let it go, friend. Herta will never come...

t_ch: 哎，真受不了…要拍快点拍，我很忙的。
t_en: Ah, you just don't give up! ...If you're taking the picture then take it. I'm busy!

t_ch: 哈哈…真是太阳打西边出来了。
t_en: Haha... Well well, I never thought I'd live to see such a day!

t_ch: 姬子…？
t_en: Himeko...?

t_ch: 姬子小姐？为什么？她可是星穹列车最尊贵的领航员，而且身高明明…呃，没事，你说了算。
t_en: Miss Himeko? Why? She is the most respected navigator on the Astral Express? Plus, her height is clearly... Ah, never mind. You're the boss.

t_ch: 呵呵，原来是想要合影…没问题。怎么样，我对准了吗？
t_en: Hehe, you want to take a picture together... No problem! Well, am I in the right spot?

t_ch: 天啊，姬子小姐永远这么优雅又善解人意。
t_en: My, my... Miss Himeko is always so elegant and understanding.
